summary: divide() returns integer-truncated result instead of a float
description:
In calculator.py, the divide(a, b) function is implemented using floor division (`a // b`) instead of true division (`a / b`). This truncates the result to an integer, dropping any fractional part.

Steps to reproduce:
  >>> from calculator import divide
  >>> divide(7, 2)
  3

Expected: divide(7, 2) should return 3.5 (a true float division), not 3.

The regression test tests/test_calculator.py::test_divide asserts divide(7, 2) == 3.5 and currently fails with 'AssertionError: 3 != 3.5'.
